hero.titrePart1: Put your savings to work on the
hero.lead: DiaspoInvest teaches you how to invest on the BRVM from France or from Africa — pick your stocks, simulate your returns, and stay fully compliant when declaring your account.

probleme.titre: Your money is losing value while you hesitate.
probleme.point1Context: the current return on standard savings accounts
probleme.point1Texte: The typical French savings account caps at 1.5% while solid African companies pay out more than 6% in annual dividends. Your money loses purchasing power every month.
probleme.point2Texte: French residents are legally required to declare any foreign securities account. Yet most diaspora investors don't know this — and risk a penalty every year.
probleme.point3Context: clear instructions for investing from the diaspora
probleme.point3Texte: There's no simple guide, no tutorial explaining how to open a brokerage account from Paris or Montreal, and what to buy first.

histoire.p1: As a student in Corporate Finance and Financial Engineering, I study European investment models every day: asset valuation, portfolio management, ETFs, derivatives.
histoire.p2: When I wanted to diversify my portfolio, I looked around. ETFs, French tax-advantaged accounts, MSCI World, European indices… everything pointed to the same Western markets. So I asked a simple question: is there anything connected to Africa?
histoire.p3: The West African stock exchange has existed since 1998. It brings together the leading companies of eight UEMOA countries and pays dividends every year. Yet almost no one talks about it, even within the diaspora.
histoire.p4: With my background, I had the tools to analyze this market. French-language resources built for the diaspora simply didn't exist. I created DiaspoInvest to build them.

faq.sousTitre: Direct answers, no jargon, on how to invest on the BRVM from abroad.

footer.tagline: The first platform dedicated to investing on the African stock market for the diaspora and UEMOA residents.
footer.disclaimerBas: Not affiliated with the BRVM or the CREPMF · This is not investment advice
